perturbation.original_text: 1. Defined Terms. 1.1 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Party, any entity directly or indirectly controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means (a) ownership of fifty percent (50%) (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign entity or investor in a particular jurisdiction) or more of the outstanding voting stock or other ownership interest of an entity, or (b) possession of the power to (i) elect, appoint, direct or remove fifty percent (50%) or more of the members of the board of directors or other governing body of an entity or (ii) otherwise direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of an entity by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Defined Terms. 1.1 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Party, any entity that has a business relationship with the that party, regardless of control. This includes but is not limited to, suppliers, partners, and any entity providing services. For purposes of this definition, "control" means (a) ownership of twenty percent (20%) (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign entity or investor in a particular jurisdiction) or more of the outstanding voting stock or other ownership interest of an entity, or (b) possession of the power to (i) elect, appoint, direct or remove twenty percent (20%) or more of the members of the board of directors or other governing body of an entity or (ii) otherwise direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of an entity by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of 'Affiliate' to include any entity with a business relationship with a party, regardless of control, while also changing the control to 20% ownership. This contradicts Section 6.4, which assumes the original definition of Affiliate where control requires 50% ownership or more to grant a license to Anixa under OntoChem's patents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4 License Grant. OntoChem hereby grants to Anixa a non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, transferable, worldwide license (with the right to grant and authorize sublicenses through multiple tiers) under any patents which OntoChem or any of its Affiliates own or control during the term of this Agreement, to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale and import the Lead Scaffold(s) and products that incorporate compounds from the Lead Scaffold(s). OntoChem will not incorporate any invention, discovery or other proprietary information owned by any third party into any Anixa Inventions or Deliverables without Anixa's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the definition of 'Affiliate' to include entities with any business relationship, regardless of control, while simultaneously reducing the control threshold to 20%. This directly contradicts Section 6.4, which grants a license to Anixa based on OntoChem's Affiliates as defined by the original control threshold of 50%. The altered definition broadens the scope of 'Affiliates', potentially granting licenses to entities not originally intended under Section 6.4, creating a contradiction concerning the scope of the license grant.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Research Program Payments. In consideration for OntoChem's performance of its activities under the Research Plan, Anixa will: (a) pay OntoChem 100,002 Euros in six (6) equal installments as follows: (i) 16,667 Euros within five (5) days after the Effective Date; and (ii) five (5) installments in the amount of 16,667 Euros on each one-month anniversary of the Effective Date, except that the last such payment will be due within thirty (30) days after completion of all activities under the Research Plan; and (b) reimburse OntoChem for its out-of-pocket expenses incurred in performing the Research Plan on a pass- through basis without mark-up, within thirty (30) days after delivery of an invoice therefore (including reasonable supporting documentation), provided that Anixa has approved such expenses in advance and in writing (including in regard to the selection of specific Hit Compounds to be synthesized and analyzed in biological assays). It is estimated that OntoChem's out-of-pocket expenses under the Research Plan will include 110,000 Euros payable to Tube Pharmaceuticals GmbH as a subcontractor of OntoChem, subject to Section 2.5. (c) High-throughput screening compounds OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to acquire these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (d) Extra custom synthesis OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have synthesized these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (e) Biological testing OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have biologically test these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Research Program Payments. In consideration for OntoChem's performance of its activities under the Research Plan, Anixa will: (a) pay OntoChem 100,002 Euros in six (6) equal installments as follows: (i) 16,667 Euros within five (5) days after the Effective Date; and (ii) five (5) installments in the amount of 16,667 Euros on each one-month anniversary of the Effective Date, except that the last such payment will be due within sixty (60) days after completion of all activities under the Research Plan; and (b) reimburse OntoChem for its out-of-pocket expenses incurred in performing the Research Plan on a pass- through basis without mark-up, within fifteen (15) days after delivery of an invoice therefore (including reasonable supporting documentation), provided that Anixa has approved such expenses in advance and in writing (including in regard to the selection of specific Hit Compounds to be synthesized and analyzed in biological assays). It is estimated that OntoChem's out-of-pocket expenses under the Research Plan will include 110,000 Euros payable to Tube Pharmaceuticals GmbH as a subcontractor of OntoChem, subject to Section 2.5. (c) High-throughput screening compounds OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to acquire these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (d) Extra custom synthesis OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have synthesized these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (e) Biological testing OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have biologically test these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the payment deadlines for the last installment (60 days instead of 30) and for out-of-pocket expenses (15 days instead of 30). This contradicts section 3.4, which says payment shall be made by check or wire, failing to account of new deadlines for final payments
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 Payment Terms. Payments to OntoChem will be made by check or by wire transfer of immediately available funds to such bank account as designated in writing by OntoChem from time to time. Taxes (and any penalties and interest thereon) imposed on any payment made by Anixa to OntoChem will be the responsibility of OntoChem. The fees for the respective bank transfers will be borne by Anixa.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadlines for the last installment and out-of-pocket expenses. Specifically, the last installment is changed from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' and out-of-pocket expenses are changed from 'thirty (30) days' to 'fifteen (15) days'. This directly contradicts section 3.4, which states that payments to OntoChem will be made by check or wire transfer, implying a standard payment term without accounting for the newly introduced, differing deadlines for specific payment types, thus creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 4.5 Effects of Termination. Promptly following expiration or termination of this Agreement, OntoChem will provide Anixa with an invoice (including reasonable supporting documentation) for any pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses (including non- cancellable commitments) incurred by OntoChem in performing the Research Plan and not yet reimbursed by Anixa, and Anixa will pay such invoice within thirty (30) days after receipt thereof. In addition, if this Agreement is terminated prior to completion of the Research Program, OntoChem will promptly furnish to Anixa any Deliverable or other work product generated to date and not previously provided to Anixa, including work in process.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5 Effects of Termination. Promptly following expiration or termination of this Agreement, OntoChem will provide Anixa with an invoice (including reasonable supporting documentation) for any pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses (including non- cancellable commitments) incurred by OntoChem in performing the Research Plan and not yet reimbursed by Anixa, and Anixa will pay such invoice within fifteen (15) days after receipt thereof. In addition, if this Agreement is terminated prior to completion of the Research Program, OntoChem will promptly furnish to Anixa any Deliverable or other work product generated to date and not previously provided to Anixa, including work in process.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the payment deadlines of expenses after termination. Making it 15 days instead of 30. This creates an in-text contradiction with 3.1 part b, where it defines 15 days for reimbursement of expenses, but 3.1 defines it as 30 days after delivery of an invoice for out of pocket expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Research Program Payments. In consideration for OntoChem's performance of its activities under the Research Plan, Anixa will: (a) pay OntoChem 100,002 Euros in six (6) equal installments as follows: (i) 16,667 Euros within five (5) days after the Effective Date; and (ii) five (5) installments in the amount of 16,667 Euros on each one-month anniversary of the Effective Date, except that the last such payment will be due within sixty (60) days after completion of all activities under the Research Plan; and (b) reimburse OntoChem for its out-of-pocket expenses incurred in performing the Research Plan on a pass- through basis without mark-up, within fifteen (15) days after delivery of an invoice therefore (including reasonable supporting documentation), provided that Anixa has approved such expenses in advance and in writing (including in regard to the selection of specific Hit Compounds to be synthesized and analyzed in biological assays). It is estimated that OntoChem's out-of-pocket expenses under the Research Plan will include 110,000 Euros payable to Tube Pharmaceuticals GmbH as a subcontractor of OntoChem, subject to Section 2.5. (c) High-throughput screening compounds OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to acquire these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (d) Extra custom synthesis OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have synthesized these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions. (e) Biological testing OntoChem will forward a commercial proposal to have biologically test these compounds at the sole discretion of Anixa. Both parties will agree on payment conditions.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the payment deadline for expenses after termination from 30 days to 15 days. This directly contradicts section 3.1 (b), which specifies a 15-day payment term for out-of-pocket expenses incurred during the research plan. The modification introduces a different payment term (15 days) for a specific circumstance (termination), while section 3.1 (b) already defines a 15 day payment schedule, creating a conflict within the agreement regarding the expenses to be paid. The contradictory deadlines, meet the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction.
